title: Lesson 30 — The Recluse

words.definition: a person who lives in seclusion and avoids others
words.example: The famous author became a recluse in his final years.

words.definition: a person who lives alone, apart from society
words.example: The old hermit had not left his mountain in decades.

words.definition: the state of being alone, often by choice
words.example: She craved the solitude of an empty studio.

words.definition: the state of being private and away from other people
words.example: They retreated to the seclusion of a remote cabin.

words.definition: distant or reserved; unwilling to become involved
words.example: He kept aloof from the office gossip.

words.definition: not wanting to communicate with others; introverted
words.example: After the loss he grew quiet and withdrawn.

words.definition: narrow-minded and cut off from outside influence
words.example: The insular village distrusted every stranger.

words.definition: to seclude or shut away from the world
words.example: He cloistered himself with his books for the summer.
The jury was sequestered until it reached a verdict.

words.definition: a person who dislikes and avoids other people
words.example: The old misanthrope chased children off his lawn.

words.definition: given to examining one's own thoughts and feelings
words.example: Long walks left her quiet and introspective.
A forlorn dog waited by the shuttered house.

story: The novelist had become a [[recluse|recluse]], living like a [[hermit|hermit]] at the edge of the moor. He prized his [[solitude|solitude]] and guarded the [[seclusion|seclusion]] of his cottage, [[aloof|aloof]] from the village and increasingly [[withdrawn|withdrawn]]. Some called him [[insular|insular]]; he preferred to [[cloister|cloister]] himself with his manuscripts and would [[sequester|sequester]] himself for weeks. The children thought him a [[misanthrope|misanthrope]], but he was really only [[introspective|introspective]] — and, on the longest winter nights, a little [[forlorn|forlorn]].
